title: Corporate Housing in Cleveland, Ohio
metaDescription: Need corporate housing in Cleveland, OH? Compare furnished short-term options, typical costs, and how our rental homes can help. Contact our team today.
answerBox: Corporate housing in Cleveland is a furnished, all-inclusive rental typically booked for 30 days or longer for relocations, projects, or temporary assignments. It costs more per month than a standard unfurnished lease. Rent Finder Cleveland's standard homes are unfurnished, longer-term rentals, but flexible or furnished arrangements can sometimes be discussed directly.

sections.h2: What is corporate housing, and how does it work in Cleveland?
body: Corporate housing is a furnished rental — usually a house, duplex unit, or apartment — leased for a set project or relocation period, commonly 30 days to several months, with utilities, basic furniture, and sometimes housewares included in one monthly rate. It exists to bridge the gap between a hotel stay and a standard 12-month lease.

In Cleveland, corporate housing is most often booked by employees relocating for jobs at large regional employers, contractors on multi-month projects, or companies housing staff during training or transition periods. Cleveland Clinic and University Hospitals, the region's two largest employers, generate a steady stream of this kind of temporary-housing demand, as do downtown corporate offices and Case Western Reserve University-adjacent research institutions.

sections.h2: Does Rent Finder Cleveland offer corporate housing?
body: Our standard rental homes are unfurnished, long-term leases, not turnkey corporate-housing units — that is a distinct product from what we manage day to day. That said, we manage 90+ rental homes across Greater Cleveland, and lease terms, furnishing, and move-in timing can vary by property and owner, so a shorter or more flexible arrangement is sometimes possible depending on what is currently available.

If your need is a relocation, project assignment, or transition period rather than a standard 12-month lease, the most efficient path is to [contact our team](/p/book-showing) directly and describe your timeline, so we can tell you honestly whether anything in our current inventory could work — rather than us guessing at availability in an article.

sections.h2: Where our rental homes are located
body: Our portfolio is concentrated in East and Southeast Cleveland, including Slavic Village (ZIP 44105), Collinwood (44110), Glenville (44108), Fairfax/Central (44104), Hough (44103), and the Buckeye-Shaker Square area (44120), with a smaller number of homes on the West side around Cudell/Detroit-Shoreway (44102) and Old Brooklyn (44109). Fairfax (44104) sits directly adjacent to the Cleveland Clinic main campus, and Hough (44103) sits between Downtown and University Circle, which is useful context if your temporary-housing need is tied to a hospital or downtown assignment.
We also manage a smaller number of homes in Akron, Lorain, and Elyria, Ohio, outside the core Cleveland footprint.

sections.h2: What does corporate housing cost compared to a standard lease?
body: Furnished, all-inclusive corporate housing generally carries a premium over an unfurnished long-term lease of a comparable size, because the monthly rate bundles furniture, utilities, and shorter minimum stays into one price. For reference, here is how Cleveland's general rental market compares by bedroom count, alongside our own portfolio's typical unfurnished range.

sections.h2: Section 8 and long-term alternatives worth knowing about
body: If your stay in Cleveland turns out to be longer-term rather than a short corporate assignment, it's worth knowing that every home we manage accepts Housing Choice Vouchers (Section 8) and is HUD-inspection-ready — a detail that matters if a household member qualifies for a voucher during a longer relocation. See our [Cleveland Section 8 guide](/section-8-housing-cleveland-oh/) for how that program works locally.

For renters comparing a short furnished stay against signing a standard 12-month lease, our [houses for rent in Cleveland](/houses-for-rent-cleveland-oh/) and [furnished houses for rent](/cleveland-rentals/houses/furnished-houses-for-rent-cleveland/) pages outline both paths in more detail.

faq.q: Does Rent Finder Cleveland provide furnished corporate housing?
faq.a: Our standard homes are unfurnished, longer-term rentals rather than a dedicated corporate-housing product. Depending on the specific property and owner, a furnished or shorter-term arrangement is sometimes possible. Contact our team with your timeline and we'll tell you honestly what could work rather than guessing.

faq.q: How long is a typical corporate housing stay in Cleveland?
faq.a: Corporate housing is usually booked for 30 days or longer, often matching a relocation, project, or training assignment of a few months. It fills the gap between a hotel stay and a standard 12-month lease.

faq.q: Is corporate housing more expensive than a regular apartment or house rental?
faq.a: Generally yes, on a per-month basis, because the rate includes furniture, utilities, and a shorter minimum stay bundled into one price. A standard unfurnished lease of the same size is typically less expensive per month if you can commit to a longer term.

faq.q: What Cleveland neighborhoods are closest to Rent Finder Cleveland's rental homes?
faq.a: Our portfolio is concentrated in East and Southeast Cleveland — Slavic Village (44105), Collinwood (44110), Glenville (44108), Fairfax/Central (44104), Hough (44103), and Buckeye-Shaker (44120) — with a smaller West-side presence in Cudell/Detroit-Shoreway (44102) and Old Brooklyn (44109).

faq.q: Can I apply for a longer-term rental instead of corporate housing?
faq.a: Yes. If your relocation may extend beyond a few months, a standard lease is usually more cost-effective. You can review current listings and apply through our [online application portal](/apply) or book a showing to discuss timing.
